Description: Canal Park is a tourist and recreation-oriented district of Duluth, Minnesota, United States. Situated across the Interstate 35 freeway from Downtown Duluth, it is connected by the famous Aerial Lift Bridge to the Park Point sandbar and neighborhood.

The Aerial Lift Bridge is a landmark in the port city of Duluth, Minnesota. The span began life in 1905 as the United States' first transporter bridge. In 1929–30, the span was converted to a vertical lift bridge, and continues to operate today.
Recreational mountain featuring wintertime downhill skiing & a summertime adventure park.
Glensheen, the Historic Congdon Estate is a 20,000 square foot mansion in Duluth, Minnesota, United States, operated by the University of Minnesota Duluth as a historic house museum.

The Great Lakes Aquarium opened in 2000 and is located on the Duluth waterfront. A 501 private nonprofit, its mission is to inspire people to explore their connection to Lake Superior and waters of the world.
Jay Cooke State Park is a state park of Minnesota, United States, protecting the lower reaches of the St. Louis River.
Seasonal narrated train tours follow a historic transport corridor for short, long & themed rides.
Enger Tower is a historic stone observation tower located in Enger Park that offers panoramic views of Duluth and Lake Superior.
Leif Erikson Park features a scenic rose garden and access to the Lakewalk.
The Duluth Lakewalk is a popular waterfront trail along Lake Superior.
